rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN LAWERENCE WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/12/2021 1) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 3) If a beneficiary satisfies the requirement of Probate Code 11801, a certified copy of Letters certified within sixty (60) days and issued to the personal representative of the estate of the beneficiary, the original 13100 affidavit executed by the person(s) entitled to the beneficiary's share, or a certified copy of the Spousal/Domestic Partner Property Order issued to the beneficiary's surviving spouse/registered domestic partner must be filed before the hearing date. [LRSF 14.36 G.] 4) Please explain and file receipts in support of entry 10/12/2020 Reimbursement for Pre-Sale House Expenses of $10,703.89. 5) The caption of the proposed order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6)] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/28/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HARRYETTE B. NESTEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/2/2021 first available date. 1) Filing Objections as a Declaration is not proper procedure. Nancy Lewellen must file proper Objections and pay a filing fee. 2) Please explain why the petition requests both the 7/2/2018 and 7/1/2019 Will be admitted when the 2019 Will says, "I hereby revoke all my previous Wills and codicils." 3) Please explain and provide legal authority for why Trisha Friedeberg acting as personal representative is not a conflict of interest when it appears she also represents Matthew Nestel. 4) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.] 5) Please explain why petitioner alleges the Will waives bond when the Will does not waive bond for Trisha Friedeberg. 6) Please explain how Trisha Friedeberg has priority for appointment under Probate Code 8461. 7) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 8) Please provide legal authority why a creditor claim will be filed in the probate and not the trust. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/19/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARLA RODRIGUEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Authority To Sell Real Property Located At 12 East 132nd St Apt 2a, New York, New York And 2) Authority To Enter Into An Exclusive Right To Sell Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 2) Attorney should be prepared to provide legal authority and cite specific code sections in support of the petition at the 6/23/2021 hearing. Petition requests authority to sell real property in New York but no legal has been authority provided. Petition does not provide any legal authority regarding whether real property located outside California can be included in a California conservatorship estate. No legal authority provided for whether the New York "co-op" is real property or personal property [Probate Code 2400, 2600, and LRSF 14.30 E. 1)]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTINE MARGARET STEINKELLNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/21/2021 1) In addition to all persons mentioned in the will, all heirs must be listed even if a decedent died testate. If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C. 1)] The date of death must be specified for all deceased devisees and heirs. [LRSF 14.18 D. 1)]. 2) Must file Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). 3) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 4) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/6/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SALLY A. MCDONNELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will. No appearance required. No conformed copy of signed order will be returned (no self-addressed stamped envelope provided). Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 06/28/2022. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 07/13/2022.
